purpose: Extract PA specifications for Section 2 (Architecture) and Section 9 (Healthcare)
key_findings: PA Name: Healthcare HIPAA Compliance PA v1.0.0
1024-dimensional embedding using mistral-embed model
Fidelity threshold: 0.65 (block), 0.35-0.70 (RAG), <0.35 (Human)
Full instantiation text: 1324 lines including purpose, scope, boundaries, escalation criteria
Constitutional constraints with 8 absolute prohibitions against PHI disclosure

purpose: Extract 54-attack validation statistics for Section 3 (Methodology) and Section 4 (Results)
key_findings: 54 attacks tested across 6 model configurations
CRITICAL FINDING: Mistral Small + TELOS = 0% ASR, Mistral Large + TELOS = 0% ASR
Baselines: Raw Mistral Small 30.8% ASR, Raw Mistral Large 43.9% ASR
System Prompt: Mistral Small 11.1% ASR, Mistral Large 3.7% ASR
Attack levels: L1-L5 (Naive, Intermediate, Advanced, Expert, Semantic Optimization)
Constraint targets: topic_boundary, role_boundary, privacy_boundary, impl_boundary, accuracy_boundary

rationale: Section 4 (Results) depends on Section 3 (Methodology) because readers need to understand attack design before seeing ASR/VDR statistics. Section 9 (Healthcare) can be written in parallel since it is domain-specific validation.
alternatives_considered: Write Section 4 before Section 3 (rejected: results without methodology lacks context)
Integrate Section 9 into Section 4 (rejected: healthcare deserves dedicated deep dive as template for future domains)
impact: Enables parallel authoring of general validation (Sections 3-4) and healthcare validation (Section 9)

purpose: Track planning decisions for rigorous technical documentation

rationale: Section 1 provides overview and reproduction instructions. Needs validation results for 'What to Expect' section and PA config for 'System Requirements'. No dependencies on other sections.

Section 2 explains PA embedding generation, fidelity calculation, and tier escalation thresholds. Depends on Section 1 for context. Needs PA config for specifications and implementation code for algorithms.

Section 3 describes attack design, test harness, and metrics. Depends on Section 2 (readers need to understand PA before understanding how attacks target it). Needs attack library for attack taxonomy and validation protocol for test procedure.

Section 4 presents ASR/VDR statistics and attack-by-attack breakdowns. MUST follow Section 3 (results meaningless without methodology). Needs validation results for statistics and forensic traces for decision analysis.

Section 5 derives cosine similarity formulas, fidelity bounds, and proportional control equations. Depends on Section 2 for PA architecture context. Needs PA config for threshold values and embedding dimensions.

Section 6 explains JSONL telemetry format, logging points, and audit trail generation. Depends on Section 2 for tier architecture. Needs telemetry logs for examples and implementation code for logging mechanisms.

Section 7 maps TELOS to California SB 53, Colorado CAIA, EU AI Act, FDA QSR. Depends on Section 2 (architecture) and Section 4 (evidence). Needs validation results for compliance evidence, PA config for technical controls, regulatory docs for requirements.

Section 8 provides practical deployment patterns for orchestration layer, PA initialization, telemetry setup. Depends on Section 2 (architecture) and Section 6 (telemetry). Needs implementation code for examples and PA config for initialization patterns.

Section 9 is self-contained deep dive on healthcare domain. Depends on Sections 2-4 for framework understanding. Needs all healthcare-specific data sources. Can be read independently by healthcare-focused readers after reading Sections 1-4.

Section 10 outlines future domains (finance, education, legal), dual PA architectures, and scalability research. Depends on Section 9 as template for future domain validations. Needs validation results to identify gaps and opportunities.

purpose: Track planning decisions for rigorous technical documentation

evidence: Section 1 contains all required subsections: Document Purpose, Audience, How to Use, Repository Structure, System Requirements, Quick Test, Full Test, Verification Checklist, Understanding Results, Next Steps
notes: Tutorial approach with clear reproduction instructions. Includes expected outputs and troubleshooting.

evidence: Explicit bash commands provided for both quick test (5-10 min) and full test (20-30 min). System requirements, environment setup, and verification checklist included.
notes: Commands are copy-paste ready. Expected outputs documented for validation.

rationale: Structured as reproduction guide first, context second. Readers who want to validate claims immediately can jump to 1.4 (Quick Test) or 1.5 (Full Test) without reading theory.
alternatives_considered: Theory-first approach (rejected: delays validation for skeptical readers)
Results-first approach (rejected: lacks context for interpreting results)
impact: Tutorial-style structure enables multiple reader paths: Quick validation (1 hour), Full review (4-6 hours), Contribution prep (8-12 hours)

purpose: Track planning decisions for rigorous technical documentation
Document PA mathematics for Section 2 (Architecture) and Section 5 (Mathematical Proofs)
key_findings: PrimacyAttractorMath class with attractor center formula: â = (τ·p + (1-τ)·s) / ||τ·p + (1-τ)·s||
Basin radius formula: r = 2/ρ where ρ = constraint_rigidity = 1 - τ
Lyapunov function: V(x) = ||x - â||² measures energy/distance to attractor
calculate_cosine_similarity() and calculate_fidelity() functions for fidelity measurement
TelicFidelityCalculator with hard fidelity (basin membership) and soft fidelity (proximity)

purpose: Document intervention system for Section 2 (Architecture) and Section 8 (Implementation Patterns)
key_findings: ProportionalController implements graduated interventions: MONITOR → CORRECT → INTERVENE → ESCALATE
Thresholds: F ≥ 0.85 (monitor), 0.70 ≤ F < 0.85 (correct), 0.50 ≤ F < 0.70 (intervene), F < 0.50 (escalate)
Proportional control law: F = K·e_t where correction force scales with error signal
Intervention types: Context injection (reminder), Regeneration (with constraints), Anti-meta suppression
Epsilon thresholds scale with constraint_tolerance: ε_min = 0.1 + 0.3τ, ε_max = 0.5 + 0.4τ

purpose: Document dual PA architecture for Section 2 (Architecture) and Section 10 (Future Research)
key_findings: Dual PA: User PA (WHAT to discuss) + AI PA (HOW to help)
Lock-on derivation: AI PA computed from User PA for automatic alignment
Intent-to-role mapping: detect_user_intent() → derive_ai_pa_from_user_pa()
check_dual_pa_fidelity() checks both user_fidelity and ai_fidelity with independent thresholds
Fallback to single PA mode if correlation < 0.2

rationale: Section 2 will reference actual implementation files from telos/core/ with line numbers for verifiability. This proves claims are backed by working code, not theoretical designs.
alternatives_considered: Abstract architectural diagrams only (rejected: lacks reproducibility)
Pseudocode representations (rejected: less trustworthy than actual code)
impact: Readers can verify architecture claims by reading actual implementation. Supports peer review readiness quality gate.
